info.title: Brilliant Directories API
info.description:
Universal API for Brilliant Directories websites. Manage members, content, leads, email templates, categories, and more. Works with any BD-powered site â€” just provide your site URL and API key.

## Authentication
All requests require an X-Api-Key header. Keys are created in BD Admin > Developer Hub > Generate API Key (see https://support.brilliantdirectories.com/support/solutions/articles/12000088768).

## Rate Limiting
Default limit: **100 requests per 60 seconds per API key.**

Customers can request a higher limit from Brilliant Directories support â€” any value between **100 and 1,000 requests per minute.** This is set server-side by BD on request; it is NOT a self-service setting in the BD admin. If you expect heavy API usage, contact BD support before bulk operations.

When the limit is exceeded, the API returns **HTTP 429 Too Many Requests**. Clients and AI agents should:
- For bulk operations (imports, batch updates, mass exports), pace requests below the configured limit
- On receiving 429, back off and retry after at least 60 seconds
- For known-large jobs, either ask BD support to raise the limit first OR process in batches with delays

The /api/v2/token/verify endpoint can be called before bulk jobs to confirm credentials work.

## Pagination
List endpoints use cursor-based pagination via limit + page params. Default page size is 25, max 100.

## Error Format
All errors: { "status": "error", "message": "..." } with standard HTTP codes (400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 404 not found, 429 rate limited, 500 server error).

get.description:
Verify that your API key is valid and check rate limit status.

**Parameter interactions:**
- Call at the start of a session to confirm the API key is valid BEFORE issuing real calls â€” saves rate-limit budget on key-config errors

**Returns:** `{ status: "success"|"error", message: ... }` â€” BD's standard response envelope.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` â€” the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Get a paginated list of all members. Supports filtering by any user field and sorting.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort.

**Enums:** `property_operator`: `=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`; `order_type`: `ASC`, `DESC`.

**See also:** `getUser` (single record by ID), `searchUsers` (keyword search).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record is the full resource object.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) â€” DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

active.description:
The status of the user after the account is created.

Valid values:
  1 = Not Active - Account Still Requires Activation
  2 = Active - Account is Live
  3 = Canceled - Sets User as Canceled Status
  4 = On Hold - Account Requires Moderation
  5 = Past Due - Sets User as Past Due Status

post.description:
Create a new user record. Writes live data.

**Required:** `email`, `password`, `subscription_id`.

**Enums:** `active`: `1`=Not Active, `2`=Active, `3`=Canceled, `4`=On Hold, `5`=Past Due; `listing_type`: `Individual`=Individual Person, `Company`=Company / Organization; `verified`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No; `nationwide`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `email`, `password`, `subscription_id` are all required together â€” `subscription_id` must be an existing membership plan (discover via `listMembershipPlans`)
- `profession_id` + `services` â€” top-level category and comma-separated sub-category IDs
- `auto_geocode=1` requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" site feature enabled
- `auto_image_import=1` â€” **fetches external image URLs and saves them to BD site storage.** Applies to the `profile_photo`, `logo`, and `cover_photo` fields when populated with external URLs (e.g. URLs from a web-scrape). Without this flag, BD stores the external URL as-is; images may break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying image URLs from any external source** â€” only omit if the user explicitly asks to keep the external reference without local copy. Processing delay: several minutes after create.

**See also:** `updateUser` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` â€” includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) â€” DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

put.description:
Update any user field. Supports special actions: member_tag_action + member_tags, credit_action + credit_amount, images_action, services, auto_geocode.

**Required:** `user_id`.

**Enums:** `active`: `1`=Not Active, `2`=Active, `3`=Canceled, `4`=On Hold, `5`=Past Due; `listing_type`: `Individual`=Individual Person, `Company`=Company / Organization; `verified`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No; `nationwide`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `member_tag_action=1` + `member_tags` â€” apply tag changes (comma-separated tag IDs from `listTags`)
- `credit_action` (`add`/`deduct`/`override`) + `credit_amount` â€” adjust member credit balance
- `images_action` â€” remove stored images: `remove_all`, `remove_cover_image`, `remove_logo_image`, `remove_profile_image`
- `services` format: `category=>service1,service2` (category ID, then comma-separated service IDs)
- `auto_geocode=1` requires the "Pretty URLs with Google - `auto_image_import=1` â€” **fetches external image URLs and saves them to BD site storage.** Applies to the `profile_photo`, `logo`, and `cover_photo` fields when populated with external URLs (e.g. URLs from a web-scrape). Without this flag, BD stores the external URL as-is; images may break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying image URLs from any external source** â€” only omit if the user explicitly asks to keep the external reference without local copy. Processing delay: several minutes after create.
Maps" site feature enabled

**See also:** `createUser` (add new), `deleteUser` (remove permanently).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }` â€” the full updated record after changes applied.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) â€” DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a user record by ID. Destructive â€” cannot be undone via API.

**Required:** `user_id`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `delete_images=1` (optional) â€” also deletes the member's stored profile/cover/logo images from site storage

**See also:** `updateUser` (modify without removing).

**Destructive:** confirm intent with the user before bulk use. No soft-delete via API â€” records removed are not recoverable.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "record was deleted" }`. No body beyond the confirmation string.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Full-text search across members with category, location, and sorting options.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` to paginate large result sets.

**Enums:** `sort`: `reviews`, `name ASC`, `name DESC`, `last_name_asc`, `last_name_desc`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `q` â€” keyword (matches first name, last name, company, about, search_description)
- `pid` (category ID), `tid` (sub-category/service ID), `ttid` (sub-sub-category) â€” taxonomy filters, use IDs from `listTopCategories` / `listSubCategories`
- `address` + `dynamic=1` â€” proximity/geographic filtering
- `output_type=html` returns server-rendered HTML; `array` (default) returns structured JSON

**See also:** `getUser` (single record by ID), `listUsers` (full enumeration).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [...records] }`. Supports pagination fields when result set is large.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) â€” DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

post.description:
Create a new review record. Writes live data.

**Required:** `user_id`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `user_id` â€” the member being reviewed
- `rating_overall`: integer 1-5 (higher = better)
- `recommend`: `0`=No, `1`=Yes (shown as a thumbs-up recommendation flag)
- `review_status` controls initial visibility â€” default flow is `0` Pending â†’ admin review

**See also:** `updateReview` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` â€” includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

review_status.description:
Review status (integer). Authoritative values from BD admin:
  0 = Pending            (newly submitted, awaiting moderation)
  2 = Accepted           (approved and visible on the member profile)
  3 = Declined           (rejected by admin â€” not publicly visible)
  4 = Waiting for Admin  (member has accepted, now needs admin sign-off)
Note: value 1 is NOT valid â€” do not use. Normal moderation flow: 0 -> 2 (accepted) or 0 -> 3 (declined). When a member pre-accepts but admin review is still required, state 4 is used.

post.description:
Create a new click record. Writes live data.

**Required:** `user_id`, `click_type`, `click_name`, `click_from`, `click_url`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `user_id` â€” the member profile being tracked
- `click_type` â€” `link` (external), `phone` (reveal), or `email` (reveal)
- `click_from` â€” source surface: `profile_page` or `search_results`
- `click_url` â€” the URL that was clicked

Foreign-key reference to the data_types resource â€” the numeric data_id of a row returned by GET /api/v2/data_types/get.\nThere is NO fixed enum for this field. auto_image_import.description: **Auto-import images to site storage.** When any external image URL field is supplied on this single-image post (e.g. a feature image URL from a web-scrape), set `auto_image_import=1` to have BD fetch the image and save it locally. Without this flag, BD stores the URL as-is â€” images break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying external image URLs;** omit (or set `0`) only when the user explicitly wants to keep the external URL reference. post.description:
Create a new post record. Writes live data.

**Required:** `user_id`, `data_id`, `data_type`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `user_id` â€” owner; must be an existing member (discover via `listUsers` or `searchUsers`)
- `data_id` â€” post type category ID; get via `listPostTypes`
- `data_type` â€” data type classification; usually matches the post type's data type
- `post_status`: `0`=Draft (not visible), `1`=Published (public)
- Response includes both `post_id` and `post_token` â€” the token is used for sharable URLs

**See also:** `updateSingleImagePost` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` â€” includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

---

**Which endpoint to use â€” `data_type` family decides:**

Every post type in `data_categories` has a `data_type` field that classifies its family. Call `listPostTypes` or `getPostType` to see the `data_type` of your target post type, then choose:

| `data_type` value | Family | Use endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `4` | Multi-image (albums, galleries, photo-heavy listings â€” e.g. Classified, Photo Album, Property, Product) | `createMultiImagePost` |
| `9` | Single-image video | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `20` | Single-image article / event / blog / job / coupon | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `10`, `13`, `21`, `29` (and similar) | Internal admin types (Member Listings, Reviews, Specialties, Favorites) â€” NOT posts | Use the resource-specific endpoint (e.g. `createReview` for `data_type=13`) |

If you call the wrong create endpoint for a given post type, BD may accept the row but it won't render on the public site correctly.

**For "make a blog post" intent:** look up `data_categories` for `data_name` matching "blog" (commonly `data_id=14` with `data_type=20`) â†’ `createSingleImagePost` with that `data_id` + `data_type`.

**For "make a photo album" / "gallery" intent:** look up the album post type (often `data_id=10`, `data_type=4`) â†’ `createMultiImagePost` with that `data_id` + `data_type`. Photos are added separately via `createMultiImagePostPhoto` using the returned `group_id`.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

field_type.description:
Form field type. Authoritative list from BD admin form-field editor, grouped by category:

SELECT FIELDS (user picks from options):
  Checkbox       = Checkboxes (multi-select)
  Select         = Dropdown list (single-select)
  Radio          = Multiple choice radios (single-select)
  YesNo          = Yes or No toggle

TEXT INPUTS:
  Custom         = Custom HTML injection
  Email          = Email address input (validates format)
  HTML           = Section title / HTML heading (display-only)
  Button         = Submit form button
  Textbox        = Single-line text input
  textarea       = Multi-line textarea (note: lowercase)
  Url            = Website URL input

FANCY FIELDS:
  Date                              = Calendar date picker
  DateTimeLocal                     = Calendar date+time picker
  File                              = File upload
  FroalaEditor                      = Froala rich text editor (basic)
  FroalaEditorUserUpload            = Froala + user image upload
  FroalaEditorUserUploadPreMadeElem = Froala + upload + pre-made elements
  FroalaEditorAdmin                 = Froala with admin media manager access
  Tip                               = Help alert box (display-only)
  Hidden                            = Hidden field (not rendered, stores value)
  Country                           = Country dropdown
  State                             = State dropdown
  Number                            = Integer-only number input
  Password                          = Password field (masked)
  Phone                             = Phone number input
  CountryCodePhone                  = Phone input with country code
  Pricebox                          = Price input
  ReCaptcha                         = Google reCAPTCHA security
  HoneyPot                          = Hidden honeypot (anti-spam)
  Category                          = Top-level category list
  Years                             = Year picker

Note on casing: most values are TitleCase but "textarea" is lowercase â€” copy exact spelling from the enum. For most simple text collection use Textbox; for paragraphs use textarea; for emails use Email so BD validates the format.

For Checkbox / Select / Radio types, you must also supply the options list via the field_options parameter (if exposed).

search_priority.description:
Search-result display priority (integer, lower number = higher in results).
  0 = highest priority (shows at the top of public search results)
  1, 2, 3, ... = progressively lower priority (shown after lower-numbered priorities, then ordered by whatever the default sort is)
Use 0 for featured / premium membership tiers that should always appear first. Use higher numbers for standard or budget tiers. There is no upper bound.
This is NOT a 0/1 boolean â€” it is a full integer where the NUMERIC VALUE determines ordering.

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of SUB-level member categories (services). Read-only.

Sub Categories are level 2 of the 3-tier member classification (e.g., "Sushi" under "Restaurants"). Each has a `profession_id` pointing at its parent Top Category. `master_id` points at a parent Sub Category for sub-sub-category nesting (`master_id=0` = directly under a Top Category). Backed by BD's `list_services` table.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token.

**Filter/sort:** filter by `profession_id` to scope to sub-categories under one top category. Standard `property`/`property_value`/`property_operator` for other fields.

**See also:** `getSubCategory` (single by ID), `listTopCategories` (parents), `createSubCategory` (add new).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, ..., message: [...records] }`. Each record has `service_id`, `name`, `desc`, `profession_id`, `master_id`, `filename`, `keywords`, `sort_order`, `lead_price`, `image`.


---

**Member Category Hierarchy (3 levels):**

BD classifies members through a 3-level taxonomy â€” AI agents MUST understand all three to correctly create, assign, and query member categories:

| Level | Tool nicknames | Endpoint | BD internal table | Key field | Parent reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Top Category** | `listTopCategories`, `createTopCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_professions/*` | `list_professions` | `profession_id` (PK) | â€” |
| **2. Sub Category** | `listSubCategories`, `createSubCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_services/*` | `list_services` | `service_id` (PK) | `profession_id` â†’ parent Top Category; `master_id` â†’ parent Sub Category (for sub-sub nesting; 0 = direct child of Top) |
| **3. Member â†” Sub Category link** | `listMemberSubCategoryLinks`, `createMemberSubCategoryLink`, etc. | `/api/v2/rel_services/*` | `rel_services` | `rel_id` (PK) | `user_id` â†’ member; `service_id` â†’ Sub Category |

**How a member gets classified on their public profile:**
- `users_data.profession_id` â†’ points at a single Top Category (the member's primary classification; shown in URL slug)
- `users_data.services` â†’ CSV of Sub Category IDs the member is tagged with (multiple allowed; simpler than the join table)
- `rel_services` rows (Member â†” Sub Category links) â†’ used when you need per-link metadata like `avg_price`, `specialty`, `num_completed`. Optional; most sites use just the CSV field.

**Sub-sub-categories:** `createSubCategory` with `master_id=<parent service_id>` creates a Sub Category nested under another Sub Category (a "sub-sub"). `master_id=0` (default) means the Sub Category sits directly under a Top Category (the `profession_id`).

**There is NO `createProfession` or `createService` tool in this MCP** â€” those are the BD internal resource names. Use the tools named above.


_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Create a new web page on the site. Writes live data.

A WebPage is any static-ish page: the homepage (`seo_type=home`), about, contact, custom landing pages, category templates, profile templates, and search result templates. Backed by BD's `list_seo` table.

Pages are publicly accessible **immediately** once `content_active=1` (the default) and a valid `seo_type` is supplied â€” no admin action needed.

**Required:** `seo_type`, `filename`.

**Parameter guidance:**
- `seo_type` â€” page type identifier. Must be one of the fixed admin-UI values (invalid types produce unrenderable records). Common values: `home` (homepage replacement), `content` (generic static/landing page â€” BD admin default for "new page"), profile/search template types, etc. For a generic landing/static page, use `content`.
- `filename` â€” URL slug (e.g. `about-us`), not the full URL. The public URL is `<site-domain>/<filename>`.
- `content` â€” main HTML body. Supports `[widget=Name]` shortcodes AND template tokens.
- `content_active=1` (default) â€” page is live; `0` = hidden.

**See also:** `listWebPages` (enumerate), `updateWebPage` (modify), `createRedirect` (if replacing an existing URL, create a 301 to preserve SEO).

**Writes live data:** changes are immediately visible on the public site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` including `seo_id`.


**BD Admin UI â€” field label mapping:**

Some field names are opaque; this is what they're called in the BD admin page-settings screens.

*Display/layout toggles (integer 0/1 unless noted):*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `content_layout` | Full Screen Page Width |
| `section` | Hide Banner Ad Modules |
| `hide_header` | Hide Header |
| `hide_footer` | Hide Footer |
| `hide_top_right` | Hide Top Header Menu |
| `hide_header_links` | Hide Main Menu |
| `content_images` | Click-Enlarge Images (value: `click_to_enlarge` to enable) |
| `show_form` | **Apply NoIndex, NoFollow** (NOT a contact-form toggle â€” BD repurposed this column) |
| `disable_css_stylesheets` | Disable Default CSS Stylesheets |
| `disable_preview_screenshot` | Disable Screenshot Preview |
| `seo_text` | Wildcard URL Rewrite (catch-all routing) |

*SEO / meta:*

| `title` | Page Meta Title |

Paginated enumeration of TOP-level member categories. Read-only.

Top Categories are the highest level of the 3-tier member classification (e.g., "Restaurants", "Dentists"). Each record's `profession_id` is what populates `users_data.profession_id` on member records. Backed by BD's `list_professions` table.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort.

**See also:** `getTopCategory` (single by ID), `listSubCategories` (sub-categories filtered by `profession_id`), `createTopCategory` (add new).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record has `profession_id`, `name`, `desc`, `filename`, `keywords`, `icon`, `sort_order`, `lead_price`, `image`, `revision_timestamp`.

**Member Category Hierarchy (3 levels):**

BD classifies members through a 3-level taxonomy â€” AI agents MUST understand all three to correctly create, assign, and query member categories:

| Level | Tool nicknames | Endpoint | BD internal table | Key field | Parent reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Top Category** | `listTopCategories`, `createTopCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_professions/*` | `list_professions` | `profession_id` (PK) | â€” |
| **2. Sub Category** | `listSubCategories`, `createSubCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_services/*` | `list_services` | `service_id` (PK) | `profession_id` â†’ parent Top Category; `master_id` â†’ parent Sub Category (for sub-sub nesting; 0 = direct child of Top) |
| **3. Member â†” Sub Category link** | `listMemberSubCategoryLinks`, `createMemberSubCategoryLink`, etc. | `/api/v2/rel_services/*` | `rel_services` | `rel_id` (PK) | `user_id` â†’ member; `service_id` â†’ Sub Category |

**How a member gets classified on their public profile:**
- `users_data.profession_id` â†’ points at a single Top Category (the member's primary classification; shown in URL slug)
- `users_data.services` â†’ CSV of Sub Category IDs the member is tagged with (multiple allowed; simpler than the join table)
- `rel_services` rows (Member â†” Sub Category links) â†’ used when you need per-link metadata like `avg_price`, `specialty`, `num_completed`. Optional; most sites use just the CSV field.

**Sub-sub-categories:** `createSubCategory` with `master_id=<parent service_id>` creates a Sub Category nested under another Sub Category (a "sub-sub"). `master_id=0` (default) means the Sub Category sits directly under a Top Category (the `profession_id`).

**There is NO `createProfession` or `createService` tool in this MCP** â€” those are the BD internal resource names. Use the tools named above.


_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._
